applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile(mode='default')
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile with mode='default' to the ModelNew class. This fused the post-convolution operations (MaxPool2d + Hardtanh + Mean + Tanh) into efficient Triton kernels while keeping the ConvTranspose2d using MIOpen's optimized implementation.

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton kernel for full fusion
attempted_but_failed.reason: Manual Triton kernel implementing ConvTranspose2d + MaxPool2d + Hardtanh + Mean + Tanh was slower (9.12ms) than PyTorch reference (7.86ms). The nested loops and complex indexing in Triton resulted in inefficient code generation.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with Triton kernel wrapper
attempted_but_failed.reason: Combining torch.compile with custom Triton kernel caused compatibility issues and correctness failures.

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') is the most effective optimization for this problem on AMD MI355X
ConvTranspose2d dominates execution time (79%) and is already using MIOpen's highly optimized assembly kernel
Manual Triton kernels for ConvTranspose2d are not competitive with MIOpen
The remaining 21% (post-conv operations) are already well-optimized by torch.compile's fusion
Limited headroom for further optimization since the dominant operation is library-optimized
